senses.definition: a skin condition in which small red spots appear on the face or the neck, mainly affecting teenagers
a rare disease where painful blisters form on the skin and inside the mouth due to the body's immune system attacking healthy cells

examples.x: Medications help manage pemphigus symptoms.
Careful daily routines prevent infections in pemphigus blisters.
Dermatologists specialize in treating pemphigus.
Support groups offer help for pemphigus patients.

senses.definition: an autoimmune condition characterized by the sudden onset of hair loss in well-defined patches on the scalp or other parts of the body

examples.x: Alopecia areata causes sudden hair loss in patches, often on the scalp.
The person noticed small, round bald spots, a sign of alopecia areata.
Some individuals with alopecia areata experience regrowth of hair over time.
Dermatologists can diagnose and treat alopecia areata.
Wearing hats or scarves may be chosen by those with alopecia areata for coverage.

senses.definition: a chronic skin disorder causing thick, red, and scaly patches due to rapid skin cell overproduction

examples.x: Psoriasis results in red and scaly patches on the skin.
The dermatologist diagnosed her with psoriasis.
Certain medications can help manage psoriasis symptoms.
Psoriasis patches often appear on the elbows, knees, and scalp.
Individuals with psoriasis may experience itching and discomfort.

senses.definition: a chronic skin condition causing red, itchy rashes often starting in childhood

examples.x: The baby's red, itchy patches were signs of atopic dermatitis.
With atopic dermatitis, moisturizers play a key role in managing dry skin.
To relieve symptoms, the doctor prescribed atopic dermatitis patients corticosteroid creams.
Scratching worsens atopic dermatitis symptoms.
Atopic dermatitis symptoms can worsen if scratching persists.

senses.definition: a condition characterized by episodic narrowing of the blood vessels in the fingers and toes, resulting in reduced blood flow and color changes in the affected areas
raised, itchy welts on the skin, often triggered by factors like allergies or stress

examples.x: After eating shellfish, she developed hives on her arms and face.
Stress can sometimes lead to the sudden appearance of hives on the skin.
The child broke out in hives after coming into contact with poison ivy.
Allergies to certain foods may cause hives in some individuals.
Sun exposure can sometimes trigger hives in sensitive individuals.

senses.definition: a genetic skin condition characterized by blistering and fragility of the skin
a chronic skin condition that causes redness, swelling, and small bumps similar to acne
a chronic skin condition that causes painful, inflamed nodules and abscesses in the skin's sweat glands
a rare autoimmune disease that causes skin and tissue hardening, affecting internal organs

examples.x: The tightness in her fingers was a symptom of scleroderma.
Managing symptoms is crucial for individuals with scleroderma.

senses.definition: a skin condition characterized by the loss of pigmentation, resulting in white patches on the skin due to the destruction of melanocytes

examples.x: The singer embraced her vitiligo, promoting self-acceptance.
Sunscreen is essential for those with vitiligo to protect sensitive areas.
Michael Jackson famously had vitiligo, leading to changes in his skin color.

senses.definition: a genetic skin disorder characterized by the formation of thick, dry, and scaly skin, often resembling fish scales

examples.x: Moisturizers help manage the dryness associated with ichthyosis.
Warm baths help soften the scales and ease discomfort in ichthyosis.
Individuals with ichthyosis may require regular exfoliation to reduce scaling.

senses.definition: a rare genetic disorder that affects the nails and can cause thickened, abnormally shaped nails, as well as other skin and oral abnormalities
a small, painful blister that typically appears on or around the lips, caused by the herpes simplex virus

examples.x: She applied a cream to soothe the discomfort of the cold sore on her lip.
Cold sores can be triggered by stress or exposure to sunlight.
The small blister on his lip was identified as a cold sore.
The pharmacist recommended antiviral medication to treat the cold sore.
Cold sores caused by the herpes simplex virus can recur periodically.

senses.definition: a swollen area on the skin filled with liquid, caused by constant rubbing or by burning

examples.x: A blister is a small pocket of fluid that forms on the skin, typically caused by friction, burns, or other injuries.
Blisters can vary in size and are often filled with clear fluid, though they can also contain blood or pus if infected.
Common causes of blisters include wearing ill-fitting shoes, repetitive friction from tools or sports equipment, or exposure to heat.
To treat a blister, it's important to keep it clean, avoid popping it if possible to prevent infection, and protect it with a bandage or padding.
In severe cases, large or infected blisters may require medical attention to prevent complications and promote healing.

senses.definition: a rough, scaly patch on the skin caused by too much sun exposure and could turn into skin cancer

examples.x: Actinic keratosis looks like a dry, rough patch on your skin.
Sunscreen helps prevent the development of actinic keratosis.
Actinic keratosis is more common in fair-skinned individuals.
Dermatologists can treat actinic keratosis to reduce the risk of skin cancer.
Using protective clothing like hats helps prevent actinic keratosis on the face and neck.

senses.definition: a painful, swollen cluster of connected boils on the skin, typically caused by a bacterial infection

examples.x: The nurse found a painful carbuncle during the exam.
Warm compresses can ease discomfort from a carbuncle.
Antibiotics treat the bacterial infection causing a carbuncle.
A painful, swollen area may be a sign of a carbuncle.
Over-the-counter pain relievers can help with carbuncle discomfort.

senses.definition: a very common skin condition that causes one's skin to become dry, red, itchy, and bumpy
a serious injury resulting from excessive exposure to severely cold weather or things, causing the freezing of the nose, toes, fingers, etc.

examples.x: She developed frostbite on her fingers after being outside in the freezing weather for too long.
The skier was treated for frostbite on her toes after the long day on the slopes.
Symptoms of frostbite include redness followed by pale or grayish skin.
The doctor explained how to recognize the signs of frostbite to avoid serious injury.

senses.definition: a bacterial skin infection causing redness, swelling, and tenderness due to a skin break

examples.x: A cut can lead to cellulitis, causing redness and swelling.
Bacteria entering through a cut can cause cellulitis.
Elevating helps reduce swelling in cellulitis.
Warm compresses ease discomfort from cellulitis.
Red streaks may indicate cellulitis spreading and need prompt attention.

senses.definition: a chronic autoimmune disease causing inflammation and diverse symptoms like joint pain and skin rashes

examples.x: Skin rashes are common symptoms of lupus.
Fatigue is a prevalent issue for those with lupus.
Medications help manage symptoms of lupus.

senses.definition: a skin condition causing brown or gray-brown patches, often on the face, linked to hormonal changes or sun exposure

examples.x: Sunscreen is crucial in preventing melasma from worsening.
Hormonal fluctuations can contribute to the development of melasma.
The dermatologist prescribed a lightening cream to address melasma.

senses.definition: skin inflammation caused by contact with irritants or allergens, resulting in redness, itching, and in some cases blistering

examples.x: Dishwashing without gloves can cause contact dermatitis on the hands.
An itchy rash appeared on her face due to contact dermatitis from a new skincare product.
Identifying and avoiding triggers is crucial for managing contact dermatitis.
Applying a soothing cream helps ease discomfort from contact dermatitis.
Common irritants, like certain soaps, may trigger contact dermatitis in sensitive individuals.

senses.definition: a small, often hard elevation on the skin that is caused by an infection or a virus
a long-term skin condition causing red, itchy, and flaky patches, commonly on the scalp and face

examples.x: Dandruff is a mild form of seborrheic eczema affecting the scalp.
Flaky skin on the face may indicate seborrheic eczema.
Weather changes may influence the severity of seborrheic eczema.
Seborrheic eczema can cause discomfort and self-consciousness.
Dermatologists can recommend treatments for seborrheic eczema symptoms.

senses.definition: a skin condition causing small, rough bumps on arms, thighs, cheeks, or buttocks

examples.x: I manage keratosis pilaris on my thighs with regular moisturizing.
Fragrance-free lotions help soothe the redness from my keratosis pilaris.
Warm showers and gentle cleansers are crucial for my keratosis pilaris routine.
Exfoliating the skin may reduce the appearance of keratosis pilaris.
Keratosis pilaris is not harmful but can be a cosmetic concern.

senses.definition: a contagious fungal infection of the skin or scalp, characterized by circular, red, and itchy rashes with a raised, defined border

examples.x: Sharing personal items can increase the risk of contracting ringworm.
Antifungal creams are commonly used to treat ringworm.
The distinctive ring-shaped appearance is a characteristic sign of ringworm.
The doctor prescribed medication to clear up the ringworm on her scalp.
Children are often prone to getting ringworm, especially in communal settings like schools.

senses.definition: a contagious bacterial skin infection causing red sores that turn into yellowish-brown crusts

examples.x: Good hygiene practices help prevent the spread of impetigo.
Keeping fingernails short reduces the risk of scratching and spreading impetigo.

senses.definition: a viral infection causing painful sores, often around the genital or oral areas, due to the herpes simplex virus

examples.x: Herpes is a common sexually transmitted infection.
The stigma around herpes has lessened with increased awareness.
Avoiding close contact during outbreaks helps prevent herpes transmission.

senses.definition: injuries to areas of skin and underlying tissue, usually over a bony prominence, as a result of prolonged pressure on the skin
a condition characterized by inflammation and cracks at the corners of the mouth, often caused by a combination of factors including infection, dryness, and nutritional deficiencies
a general term referring to inflammation of the skin, often causing redness, itching, and various skin conditions

examples.x: Sunburn can lead to dermatitis, causing redness and discomfort.
Identifying and avoiding triggers is crucial in managing dermatitis.
Eczema is a form of dermatitis characterized by itchy, inflamed skin.
Allergic reactions can trigger dermatitis symptoms like itching and swelling.
Scratching can worsen dermatitis, leading to more irritation and redness.

senses.definition: a skin condition marked by itchy, raised welts caused by an allergic reaction or other triggers

examples.x: Stress or anxiety may exacerbate symptoms of nettle rash.
Applying a cold compress can help soothe the discomfort of nettle rash.
The distinctive red welts of nettle rash can vary in size and shape.
Common triggers for nettle rash include insect bites and certain medications.
Allergic reactions to certain foods can lead to sudden nettle rash.

senses.definition: a condition where the skin on certain parts of the body appears dimpled or lumpy, caused by underlying fat deposits and connective tissue
